 {"id":"bd-1yty1","title":"CAAM: Implement agent environment update after account switch","description":"After CAAM switches accounts, update the agent's environment variables.

## Challenge

Agent processes are already running with old credentials in their environment.
We need to inject new credentials without restarting the agent.

## Approaches

### Option A: Environment File (Recommended)
Agent reads credentials from file that CAAM updates:
1. CAAM writes new token to ~/.config/caam/current/<provider>.json
2. Agent's hook or wrapper reads this file on each API call
3. No agent restart needed

### Option B: Signal + Re-read
1. NTM sends signal to agent process
2. Agent re-reads environment/config
3. Requires agent support for this pattern

### Option C: Session Restart (Fallback)
1. Save agent context/state
2. Kill agent process
3. Spawn new agent with new environment
4. Restore context (if possible)

## Implementation for Option A

```go
func (m *CAAMManager) UpdateAgentEnvironment(pane string, provider string) error {
    // Get new credentials from caam
    creds, err := m.GetCurrentCredentials(provider)
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    
    // Write to agent's config location
    // (or rely on caam's built-in file updates)
    
    // For Claude Code: update ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env
    // For OpenAI: update OPENAI_API_KEY env
    // etc.
    
    return nil
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/caam/environment.go
- May need coordination with spawn code

 {"id":"bd-1zrjo","title":"TOON: Implement serialization wrapper supporting JSON and TOON","description":"Create abstraction layer for robot mode output serialization.

## Requirements

1. Single interface for serialization
2. Support JSON and TOON backends
3. Runtime switchable (per-command or global)
4. Handle errors gracefully (fallback to JSON)

## Implementation

```go
package serialize

type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
)

type Serializer interface {
    Serialize(v any) ([]byte, error)
    ContentType() string
}

func NewSerializer(format Format) Serializer {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        return &TOONSerializer{}
    default:
        return &JSONSerializer{}
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/serialize/serialize.go
- internal/serialize/json.go
- internal/serialize/toon.go

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Serializer interface defined with Serialize() and ContentType() methods
2. JSONSerializer implements interface using encoding/json
3. TOONSerializer implements interface calling toon binary
4. NewSerializer factory function returns correct implementation
5. Fallback to JSON when toon binary not found
6. Error wrapping provides clear context

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/serialize/serialize_test.go
- Test round-trip JSON encoding/decoding
- Test TOON encoding with mock binary
- Test fallback behavior when toon not available

 {"id":"bd-2o8r","title":"Task: Implement webhook manager and HTTP dispatcher","description":"## Overview
Create the core webhook infrastructure for dispatching HTTP requests on events.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/webhook/manager.go:
   - WebhookManager struct
   - Register(config WebhookConfig) error
   - Dispatch(event Event) error
   - Start() - begin background processing
   - Stop() - graceful shutdown

2. HTTP dispatcher:
   - Async dispatch (don't block event source)
   - Configurable timeout per webhook
   - Connection pooling for efficiency
   - Request signing (optional HMAC)

3. Retry logic:
   - Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 5 retries
   - Configurable retry policy per webhook
   - Dead letter queue for failed webhooks
   - Retry on 5xx errors, not 4xx

4. Event queueing:
   - In-memory queue with bounded size
   - Drop oldest events if queue full (log warning)
   - Batch dispatch for high-volume events

## Implementation Notes
- Use worker goroutine pool for dispatching
- Context cancellation for graceful shutdown
- Structured logging for debugging failures

## Acceptance Criteria
- Webhooks dispatch asynchronously
- Retries work with exponential backoff
- Failed webhooks logged, don't crash NTM
- Unit tests for dispatch and retry logic","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:31:23.033786619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:48:43.266930100Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:48:43.266885987Z","close_reason":"Implemented webhook manager and HTTP dispatcher with all features: async dispatch, connection pooling, exponential backoff retry (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s), dead letter queue, HMAC signing, bounded event queue, worker goroutine pool. 17 tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2o8r","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2oc4s","title":"Test: Integration test for full swarm lifecycle","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AutoRespawner kill and respawn sequences, validating agent lifecycle management.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/respawner_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "context"
    "testing"
    "time"
)

func TestAutoRespawner_KillSequence(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = mockTmux
    
    tests := []struct {
        name          string
        agentType     string
        paneID        string
        expectedKeys  []string // Expected key sequences sent
    }{
        {
            name:      "claude_code_kill",
            agentType: "cc",
            paneID:    "cc_agents_1:1.0",
            expectedKeys: []string{
                "C-c",        // Interrupt
                "C-c",        // Double interrupt
                "/exit",      // Claude Code exit command
                "Enter",
            },
        },
        {
            name:      "codex_kill",
            agentType: "cod",
            paneID:    "cod_agents_1:1.0",
            expectedKeys: []string{
                "C-c",
                "exit",
                "Enter",
            },
        },
        {
            name:      "gemini_kill",
            agentType: "gmi",
            paneID:    "gmi_agents_1:1.0",
            expectedKeys: []string{
                "C-c",
                "q",  // Quit key
            },
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            mockTmux.Reset()
            
            t.Logf("Testing kill sequence for %s agent", tt.agentType)
            t.Logf("Pane: %s", tt.paneID)
            
            err := respawner.KillAgent(tt.paneID, tt.agentType)
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            sentKeys := mockTmux.GetSentKeys(tt.paneID)
            t.Logf("Keys sent: %v", sentKeys)
            t.Logf("Expected: %v", tt.expectedKeys)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectedKeys, sentKeys, "Kill sequence mismatch")
        })
    }
}

func TestAutoRespawner_ForceKill(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = mockTmux
    respawner.killTimeout = 100 * time.Millisecond
    
    // Simulate unresponsive agent
    mockTmux.SetProcessRunning("test:1.0", true)
    mockTmux.SetKillPaneResult("test:1.0", nil)
    
    t.Log("Testing force kill on unresponsive agent")
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    err := respawner.ForceKill(ctx, "test:1.0")
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    // Verify kill-pane was called
    assert.True(t, mockTmux.KillPaneCalled("test:1.0"), "kill-pane should be called")
    t.Log("Force kill successful - kill-pane was invoked")
}

func TestAutoRespawner_Respawn(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := NewMockTmuxClient()
    mockLauncher := NewMockPaneLauncher()
    
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = mockTmux
    respawner.launcher = mockLauncher
    respawner.respawnDelay = 50 * time.Millisecond
    
    paneSpec := PaneSpec{
        Index:     1,
        Project:   "/home/user/project",
        AgentType: "cc",
    }
    
    t.Logf("Testing respawn for pane %d", paneSpec.Index)
    t.Logf("Project: %s", paneSpec.Project)
    t.Logf("Agent type: %s", paneSpec.AgentType)
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    startTime := time.Now()
    
    err := respawner.Respawn(ctx, "cc_agents_1", paneSpec)
    
    elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
    t.Logf("Respawn completed in %v", elapsed)
    
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    // Verify respawn delay was respected
    assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, elapsed, respawner.respawnDelay, 
        "Should respect respawn delay")
    
    // Verify launcher was called
    assert.True(t, mockLauncher.LaunchCalled(), "Launcher should be invoked")
    t.Logf("Launch args: session=%s, pane=%v", 
        mockLauncher.LastSession(), mockLauncher.LastPaneSpec())
}

func TestAutoRespawner_RespawnWithCallback(t *testing.T) {
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner.launcher = NewMockPaneLauncher()
    
    callbackCalled := false
    var receivedEvent RespawnCompleteEvent
    
    respawner.OnComplete(func(event RespawnCompleteEvent) error {
        callbackCalled = true
        receivedEvent = event
        t.Logf("Callback received: %+v", event)
        return nil
    })
    
    paneSpec := PaneSpec{
        Index:     2,
        Project:   "/test/project",
        AgentType: "cod",
    }
    
    t.Log("Testing respawn with completion callback")
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    err := respawner.Respawn(ctx, "cod_agents_1", paneSpec)
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    assert.True(t, callbackCalled, "Callback should be invoked")
    assert.Equal(t, "cod", receivedEvent.AgentType)
    assert.Equal(t, "/test/project", receivedEvent.Project)
    
    t.Log("Callback verification complete")
}

func TestAutoRespawner_ConcurrentRespawns(t *testing.T) {
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(testLogger(t))
    respawner.tmux = NewMockTmuxClient()
    respawner.launcher = NewMockPaneLauncher()
    respawner.respawnDelay = 10 * time.Millisecond
    
    panes := []PaneSpec{
        {Index: 1, AgentType: "cc", Project: "/p1"},
        {Index: 2, AgentType: "cc", Project: "/p2"},
        {Index: 3, AgentType: "cc", Project: "/p3"},
    }
    
    t.Logf("Testing concurrent respawn of %d panes", len(panes))
    
    ctx := context.Background()
    var wg sync.WaitGroup
    errors := make(chan error, len(panes))
    
    startTime := time.Now()
    
    for _, pane := range panes {
        wg.Add(1)
        go func(p PaneSpec) {
            defer wg.Done()
            if err := respawner.Respawn(ctx, "cc_agents_1", p); err != nil {
                errors <- err
            }
        }(pane)
    }
    
    wg.Wait()
    close(errors)
    
    elapsed := time.Since(startTime)
    t.Logf("All respawns completed in %v", elapsed)
    
    for err := range errors {
        t.Errorf("Respawn error: %v", err)
    }
    
    // Should complete in parallel, not sequentially
    maxExpected := respawner.respawnDelay * 2 // Some buffer for execution
    assert.Less(t, elapsed, maxExpected, "Concurrent respawns should be parallel")
}

// Mock implementations
type MockTmuxClient struct {
    sentKeys    map[string][]string
    killCalled  map[string]bool
    mu          sync.Mutex
}

func NewMockTmuxClient() *MockTmuxClient {
    return &MockTmuxClient{
        sentKeys:   make(map[string][]string),
        killCalled: make(map[string]bool),
    }
}

func (m *MockTmuxClient) SendKeys(paneID, keys string, enter bool) error {
    m.mu.Lock()
    defer m.mu.Unlock()
    m.sentKeys[paneID] = append(m.sentKeys[paneID], keys)
    if enter {
        m.sentKeys[paneID] = append(m.sentKeys[paneID], "Enter")
    }
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements

- Log expected vs actual key sequences
- Log timing for respawn delays

- [ ] Test respawn with delay
- [ ] Test completion callback

 {"id":"bd-2ouwm","title":"Feature: Intake & Context Pack - Stage 1 of pipeline","description":"# Feature: Intake & Context Pack

**Stage 1 of the 3-stage ensemble pipeline.** Auto-generates project context before mode agents run.

## Purpose

Ensure all mode agents have rich, consistent context without manual user preparation. Reduces token waste by caching and reusing context.

## Context Pack Contents

### Auto-Generated Brief
- project name, description, languages, frameworks
- directory structure summary with entry points
- recent git activity (last 5-10 commits)
- open issues count from .beads

### User Context
- problem statement (user input)
- focus areas (specific files/packages)
- constraints (time, scope, etc.)

### Targeted Questions (If Context Thin)
When auto-generation yields incomplete context, ask 5-12 targeted questions about goals, concerns, constraints, stakeholders, and decisions.

## Caching Strategy
- Hash context pack contents
- Invalidate on: code changes, new commits, user request
- TTL: 1 hour default, configurable
- Storage: ~/.cache/ntm/context-packs/

## Logging Requirements
- Log cache hit/miss + hash
- Log detected languages/frameworks
- Log thin-context triggers + questions count

- internal/ensemble/context.go
- internal/ensemble/context_generator.go
- internal/ensemble/context_cache.go

 {"id":"bd-2oz38","title":"Unit Tests: AgentLauncher pane addressing and execution","description":"## Purpose
Unit tests for AgentLauncher covering pane addressing, command execution, and agent spawn verification.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/agent_launcher_test.go
package swarm

func TestAgentLauncher_PaneAddressing(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name     string
        session  string
        window   int
        pane     int
        expected string
    }{
        {"basic", "cc_agents_1", 1, 0, "cc_agents_1:1.0"},
        {"multi_pane", "cc_agents_1", 1, 5, "cc_agents_1:1.5"},
        {"second_window", "cod_agents_2", 2, 3, "cod_agents_2:2.3"},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            result := formatPaneAddress(tt.session, tt.window, tt.pane)
            t.Logf("[TEST] session=%s window=%d pane=%d -> %s", tt.session, tt.window, tt.pane, result)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
        })
    }
}

func TestAgentLauncher_ParsePaneAddress(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        input   string
        session string
        window  int
        pane    int
        wantErr bool
    }{
        {"cc_agents_1:1.0", "cc_agents_1", 1, 0, false},
        {"cod_agents_2:1.5", "cod_agents_2", 1, 5, false},
        {"invalid", "", 0, 0, true},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.input, func(t *testing.T) {
            session, window, pane, err := parsePaneAddress(tt.input)
            
            if tt.wantErr {
                assert.Error(t, err)
            } else {
                require.NoError(t, err)
                t.Logf("[TEST] Parsed %s -> session=%s window=%d pane=%d", tt.input, session, window, pane)
                assert.Equal(t, tt.session, session)
                assert.Equal(t, tt.window, window)
                assert.Equal(t, tt.pane, pane)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestAgentLauncher_LaunchAgent(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{t: t}
    launcher := NewAgentLauncher(mockTmux)
    
    tests := []struct {
        agentType   string
        expectedCmd string
    }{
        {"cc", "cc"},
        {"cod", "cod"},
        {"gmi", "gmi"},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.agentType, func(t *testing.T) {
            err := launcher.LaunchAgent("test_session:1.0", tt.agentType)
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Agent %s launched with command: %s", tt.agentType, mockTmux.lastCommand)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectedCmd, mockTmux.lastCommand)
        })
    }
}

func TestAgentLauncher_WaitForReady(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        captureSequence: []string{
            "Starting...",
            "Loading...",
            "Claude Opus 4.5 ready >",
        },
    }
    
    launcher := NewAgentLauncher(mockTmux)
    launcher.readyTimeout = 2 * time.Second
    launcher.pollInterval = 100 * time.Millisecond
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Waiting for agent ready")
    
    err := launcher.WaitForReady("test_session:1.0", "cc")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    
    t.Logf("[TEST] Agent ready after %d capture calls", mockTmux.captureCount)
}
```

 {"id":"bd-31e1f","title":"Feature: Robot Diagnose Command (--robot-diagnose)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-diagnose <session>` command that provides a single comprehensive
health check answering "what's wrong and how do I fix it?"

## Background

Currently diagnosing swarm health requires:
1. Loop through each pane
2. Capture and grep output for error patterns
3. Check process state
4. Interpret results
5. Decide on action

This is tedious and error-prone. A single diagnose command should do all of this.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
ntm --robot-diagnose myproject
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T23:15:00Z",
  "overall_health": "degraded",  // "healthy", "degraded", "critical"
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 16,
    "healthy": 12,
    "rate_limited": 2,
    "unresponsive": 1,
    "crashed": 1
  },
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16],
    "rate_limited": [4, 10],
    "unresponsive": [15],
    "crashed": [1]
  },
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "pane": 4,
      "status": "rate_limited",
      "action": "wait_or_caam",
      "reason": "Output contains 'You've hit your limit'",
      "reset_time": "4pm ET",  // parsed from output if available
      "auto_fixable": false
    },
    {
      "pane": 10,
      "status": "rate_limited",
      "action": "wait_or_caam",
      "reason": "Output contains 'You've hit your limit'",
      "auto_fixable": false
    },
    {
      "pane": 15,
      "status": "unresponsive",
      "action": "kill_restart",
      "reason": "No output for 312 seconds, process state: sleeping",
      "auto_fixable": true,
      "fix_command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 15"
    },
    {
      "pane": 1,
      "status": "crashed",
      "action": "restart_cc",
      "reason": "Shell prompt visible, no agent process",
      "auto_fixable": true,
      "fix_command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 1"
    }
  ],
  "auto_fix_available": true,
  "auto_fix_command": "ntm --robot-diagnose myproject --fix"
}
```

## Health Classification Logic

| Status | Detection Criteria |
|--------|-------------------|
| healthy | Agent process running, recent output, no error patterns |
| rate_limited | Output contains rate limit patterns |
| unresponsive | No output for >5min AND process running |
| crashed | No agent process OR zombie process |
| unknown | Cannot determine (provide raw data) |

## Flags

- `--fix`: Automatically fix what can be fixed (crashed/unresponsive, not rate limits)
- `--json`: (default) JSON output
- `--brief`: Just summary, no per-pane details
- `--pane=N`: Diagnose specific pane only

## Why This Matters

Reduces "what's wrong?" from a 5-minute manual inspection to a 1-second command.
The recommendations with fix commands enable scripted recovery.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:16:03.415109604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:18:25.661480205Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:18:25.660292387Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-diagnose command with health classification, recommendations, and auto-fix support.  {"id":"bd-31r17","title":"Feature: DCG guard integration for destructive commands","description":"## Overview
Use DCG as an external safety guard for dangerous shell commands initiated via NTM (and in robot workflows).

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the **harmonized** robot mode patterns for tool bridges:
- **Naming**: `--robot-dcg-check` (tool prefix: dcg, no inline value)
- **Command param**: `--command=CMD` (standard modifier flag)
- **Context param**: `--context=CTX` (helps DCG make better decisions)
- Consistent with `--robot-xf-search --query=Q`, `--robot-jfp-search --query=Q`

## Command Interface

```bash
# Check a command (uses --command= modifier, NOT inline value)
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf /data/backup"

# Check with context (helps DCG understand intent)
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf ./node_modules" --context="Cleaning build artifacts"

# Check with verbose output
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="git reset --hard" --verbose

# Check with working directory context
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf ." --cwd=/tmp/scratch
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
```

## Severity Levels

| Level | Description | Example Commands |
|-------|-------------|------------------|
| `critical` | Data loss, system damage | `rm -rf /`, `dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda` |
| `high` | Significant risk | `git reset --hard`, `DROP DATABASE` |
| `medium` | Moderate risk | `rm -r ./folder`, `git push --force` |
| `low` | Minor risk, likely safe | `rm file.txt`, `git stash drop` |
| `safe` | No risk detected | `ls -la`, `cat file.txt` |

## Common Rule Codes

| Rule Code | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `RECURSIVE_DELETE_ROOT` | rm -rf targeting / or system directories |
| `RECURSIVE_DELETE_OUTSIDE_PROJECT` | rm -rf outside current project tree |
| `FORCE_PUSH_PROTECTED` | git push --force to protected branch |
| `HARD_RESET` | git reset --hard without stash |
| `DROP_DATABASE` | SQL DROP DATABASE/TABLE commands |
| `DISK_OVERWRITE` | dd to block devices |
| `CHMOD_RECURSIVE_777` | chmod -R 777 (security risk) |

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default | Scope |
|------|-------------|---------|-------|
| `--command=CMD` | Command to check (required) | - | **Required** |
| `--context=CTX` | Context/intent for the command | none | Optional |
| `--cwd=DIR` | Working directory context | current dir | Optional |
| `--verbose` | Include detailed analysis in output | false | Optional |

- internal/robot/dcg_check.go

- [ ] `--verbose` optional flag for detailed analysis

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# [E2E-DCG] Robot DCG Check End-to-End Tests
# =============================================================================
# Tests the --robot-dcg-check command for DCG safety guard integration.
# Exit codes: 0=all passed, 1=test failed, 77=skipped (dcg not installed)
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-DCG]"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
SKIPPED=0

log_info()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [INFO]  $*"; }
log_pass()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [PASS]  $*"; ((PASSED++)); }
log_fail()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [FAIL]  $*"; ((FAILED++)); }
log_skip()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [SKIP]  $*"; ((SKIPPED++)); }
log_debug() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [DEBUG] $*"; }

cleanup() {
    log_info "Test summary: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_info "Starting robot dcg E2E tests"
log_info "Test timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prerequisite Check
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

if ! command -v dcg &>/dev/null; then
    log_skip "dcg not installed - skipping all tests"
    exit 77
fi

DCG_VERSION=$(dcg --version 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
log_info "dcg version: $DCG_VERSION"

if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
    log_fail "jq is required for tests"
    exit 1
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-001: Safe command should be allowed
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-001: Safe command check"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="ls -la" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.allowed == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("timestamp") and has("cwd")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "DCG-001: Safe command allowed with timestamp and cwd"
        else
            log_fail "DCG-001: Missing timestamp or cwd field"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "DCG-001: Safe command should be allowed"
    fi
else
    log_fail "DCG-001: Check did not succeed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-002: Dangerous command should be blocked
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-002: Dangerous command check"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf /" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.allowed == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "DCG-002: Dangerous command blocked"
    else
        log_fail "DCG-002: Dangerous command should be blocked"
    fi
else
    log_fail "DCG-002: Check did not succeed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-003: Severity is returned
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-003: Severity field present"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf /tmp/test" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("severity")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    SEVERITY=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.severity' 2>/dev/null)
    log_pass "DCG-003: Severity field present (value: $SEVERITY)"
else
    log_fail "DCG-003: Missing severity field"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-004: Rationale and rule_matched for blocked commands
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-004: Rationale and rule_matched for blocked command"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="git reset --hard" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.allowed == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("rationale") and has("rule_matched")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        RATIONALE=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.rationale' 2>/dev/null)
        RULE=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.rule_matched' 2>/dev/null)
        log_pass "DCG-004: Rationale and rule provided: $RULE"
    else
        log_fail "DCG-004: Missing rationale or rule_matched for blocked command"
    fi
else
    log_pass "DCG-004: Command was allowed (rationale check skipped)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-005: Context parameter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-005: Context parameter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf ./build" --context="Cleaning build artifacts" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.context == "Cleaning build artifacts"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "DCG-005: Context parameter accepted and echoed"
    else
        log_fail "DCG-005: Context not echoed in response"
    fi
else
    log_fail "DCG-005: Context parameter failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-006: CWD parameter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-006: CWD parameter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf ." --cwd=/tmp 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    CWD=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.cwd' 2>/dev/null)
    if [[ "$CWD" == "/tmp" ]]; then
        log_pass "DCG-006: CWD parameter accepted"
    else
        log_fail "DCG-006: CWD not set correctly (expected /tmp, got $CWD)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "DCG-006: CWD parameter failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-007: Verbose mode includes extra details
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-007: Verbose mode"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm file.txt" --verbose 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "DCG-007: Verbose mode works"
else
    log_fail "DCG-007: Verbose mode failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-008: Missing --command parameter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-008: Missing --command parameter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check 2>&1) || true
EXIT_CODE=$?
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"
log_debug "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.error' 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "command.*required"; then
        log_pass "DCG-008: Missing command correctly rejected"
    else
        log_fail "DCG-008: Error message should mention command is required"
    fi
elif echo "$RESULT" | grep -qi "command.*required"; then
    log_pass "DCG-008: Missing command correctly rejected (stderr)"
else
    log_fail "DCG-008: Should require --command"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-009: Command is echoed in response
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-009: Command echoed in response"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="echo hello" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.command == "echo hello"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "DCG-009: Command echoed correctly"
else
    log_fail "DCG-009: Command not echoed in response"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-010: DCG version included
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-010: DCG version in response"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="ls" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("dcg_version")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    VERSION=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.dcg_version' 2>/dev/null)
    log_pass "DCG-010: DCG version included ($VERSION)"
else
    log_fail "DCG-010: Missing dcg_version field"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-011: Suggestion field for blocked commands
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-011: Suggestion for blocked command"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf /important" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.allowed == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("suggestion")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        SUGGESTION=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.suggestion' 2>/dev/null)
        log_pass "DCG-011: Suggestion provided: $SUGGESTION"
    else
        log_pass "DCG-011: No suggestion (optional field)"
    fi
else
    log_pass "DCG-011: Command was allowed (suggestion check skipped)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-012: Agent hints with safer_alternative
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-012: Agent hints with safer_alternative"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf /data" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.allowed == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("_agent_hints")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "DCG-012: Agent hints present for blocked command"
    else
        log_pass "DCG-012: No agent hints (optional)"
    fi
else
    log_pass "DCG-012: Command allowed, hints check skipped"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-013: Exit codes follow standard
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-013: Exit code verification"

# Test exit code 0 for successful check (safe command)
ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="ls" >/dev/null 2>&1
EXIT_CODE=$?
if [[ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]]; then
    log_pass "DCG-013a: Exit code 0 for successful check"
else
    log_fail "DCG-013a: Expected exit code 0, got $EXIT_CODE"
fi

# Test exit code 1 for missing required flag
ntm --robot-dcg-check >/dev/null 2>&1 || EXIT_CODE=$?
if [[ "${EXIT_CODE:-0}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    log_pass "DCG-013b: Exit code 1 for missing required flag"
else
    log_fail "DCG-013b: Expected exit code 1, got ${EXIT_CODE:-0}"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-014: Various severity levels
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-014: Severity level variations"

# Safe command
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="cat /etc/hosts" 2>&1) || true
SEVERITY=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.severity' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
log_debug "cat /etc/hosts severity: $SEVERITY"

# Medium risk command
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -r ./temp" 2>&1) || true
SEVERITY=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.severity' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
log_debug "rm -r ./temp severity: $SEVERITY"

# High risk command
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="git push --force" 2>&1) || true
SEVERITY=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.severity' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
log_debug "git push --force severity: $SEVERITY"

log_pass "DCG-014: Severity level handling verified"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test DCG-015: Combined options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test DCG-015: Combined options"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-dcg-check --command="rm -rf ./build" --context="CI cleanup" --cwd=/tmp/project --verbose 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.context == "CI cleanup" and .cwd == "/tmp/project"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "DCG-015: Combined options work correctly"
    else
        log_fail "DCG-015: Combined options not reflected correctly"
    fi
else
    log_fail "DCG-015: Combined options failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final Summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "========================================="
log_info "Final Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
log_info "========================================="

if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
fi

log_info "All robot dcg E2E tests passed!"
exit 0
```

- Get notified in Slack when agents complete work
- Trigger CI pipelines when all agents finish
- Alert PagerDuty when errors occur
- Build custom dashboards from live data
- Integrate with existing DevOps tooling

1. Configurable webhook endpoints per event type
2. Built-in formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams
3. Retry logic with exponential backoff
4. Event filtering by session, agent type, severity
5. Payload templates with variable substitution

## Event Types
- agent.started: Agent session created
- agent.completed: Agent finished task
- agent.error: Agent encountered error
- agent.idle: Agent became idle
- session.created: New NTM session
- session.ended: Session terminated
- bead.assigned: Bead assigned to agent
- bead.completed: Bead marked done

    formatter: slack

      session: myproject*
      severity: [warning, error, critical]
  - url: https://my-api.com/ntm-events
    events: [*]
    formatter: json

## Success Criteria
- Webhooks fire reliably on configured events
- Built-in formatters produce valid Slack/Discord messages
- Failures don't block NTM operation
- Easy to configure without code changes

- internal/webhook/formatters/ (new - Slack, Discord, JSON formatters)
- internal/config/webhooks.go (new - webhook config parsing)
